name: Charles Mingus
description: American jazz double bassist, composer and bandleader (1922–1979)
abstract: Charles Mingus Jr. (April 22, 1922 – January 5, 1979) was an American jazz upright bassist, composer, bandleader, pianist, and author.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom McIntosh
about_quotes.evidence: I’m starting a new tentet. I want you in that band... So, sure enough, one day Mingus called me... I go in and I’m looking for Charlie Mingus’ great music... We look at each other... So, he brings his hand down and we start to play.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom McIntosh — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Elvin Jones
about_quotes.evidence: And then Mingus called me and he said, “We’re going out” and it was J.R Montrose, Teddy Charles and Mingus and myself. So that’s that quartet
about_quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: I had Mingus in the band there... Charlie Mingus was playing bass with me. At the Downbeat.

about_quotes.speaker: Slam Stewart
about_quotes.evidence: I met Charles Mingus out in Los Angeles when I went out there with Slim back in '39.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: because I worked with Mingus two times, in 1964 and again in ’65.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: From Mingus, to Herbie Mann... I think that was the most creative gig that I’ve ever had, the one with the Charles Mingus band... We worked at the Monterey Jazz Festival... I stayed with the band work for about three to four months.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
